stem: A particle moves along a straight line so that its velocity at time \( t \) seconds is \( v(t) = [[m]](t - ([[c]])) \) meters per second, for \( [[a]] \le t \le [[b]] \). Find the total distance (in meters) traveled by the particle over this interval.
solution: Since \( v \) changes sign at \( t=[[c]] \), the total distance is \( \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]}|v(t)|\,dt = \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]}\bigl|[[m]](t-([[c]]))\bigr|\,dt = [[answer]] \), larger than the net displacement.
distractor_rationales: This is the net displacement \( \int v\,dt \), not the total distance.
This is the magnitude of the net displacement, still less than the distance.
This forgets the factor \( \tfrac12 \) from integrating a linear speed.
This drops the speed multiplier \( [[m]] \).
This is off by \( [[m]] \) from the correct distance.

stem: A particle moves along a straight line so that its velocity at time \( t \) seconds is \( v(t) = [[m]](t - ([[c]])) \) meters per second, for \( [[a]] \le t \le [[b]] \). Find the particle's DISPLACEMENT (net change in position, in meters) over this interval.
solution: The displacement is the net (signed) integral of velocity: \( \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]} [[m]](t-([[c]]))\,dt = [[answer]] \); this is smaller in magnitude than the total distance, which would instead integrate \( |v(t)| \).
distractor_rationales: This is the total distance traveled (integrating \( |v| \)), not the net displacement.
This takes the absolute value of the correct displacement, discarding its sign.
This has the wrong sign for the displacement.
This is off by \( [[m]] \) from the correct displacement.
This is off by \( [[m]] \) from the correct displacement.

stem: A particle moves along a straight line so that its velocity at time \( t \) seconds is \( v(t) = [[k]](t - [[p]])(t - [[q]]) \) meters per second, for \( [[a]] \le t \le [[b]] \). The velocity changes sign at both \( t = [[p]] \) and \( t = [[q]] \). Find the total distance (in meters) traveled by the particle over this interval.
solution: Because \( v \) changes sign twice, in \( ([[a]],[[p]]) \), \( ([[p]],[[q]]) \), and \( ([[q]],[[b]]) \), the total distance is \( \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]}\left|[[k]](t-[[p]])(t-[[q]])\right|dt = [[answer]] \), found by summing the (positive) areas over each of the three sub-intervals.
distractor_rationales: This is a nonsensical antiderivative that does not correspond to the correct piecewise integral.
This only counts about half the sub-intervals' contribution.
This double-counts the total distance.
This is off by \( [[k]] \) from the correct total distance.
This is off by \( [[k]] \) from the correct total distance.

stem: A particle's position on a line at time \( t \) seconds is \( x(t) = \dfrac{[[m]]}{2}(t-[[c]])^{2} + [[x0]] \) meters, for \( [[a]] \le t \le [[b]] \). Find the total distance (in meters) traveled by the particle over this interval.
solution: The velocity is \( v(t) = x'(t) = [[m]](t-[[c]]) \), which changes sign at \( t=[[c]] \). The total distance is \( \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]}|v(t)|\,dt = \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]}\bigl|[[m]](t-[[c]])\bigr|\,dt = [[answer]] \); the initial position \( [[x0]] \) has no effect on distance traveled.
distractor_rationales: This is \( |x([[b]])-x([[a]])| \), the magnitude of the net displacement, not the total distance.
This is the net (signed) displacement, not the total distance.
This drops the speed multiplier \( [[m]] \).
This incorrectly lets the initial position \( [[x0]] \) affect the distance traveled.
This incorrectly lets the initial position \( [[x0]] \) affect the distance traveled.
